edges.description: Unstable myelin becomes vulnerable to brief pressure or stretch, leading to focal conduction slowing or block at entrapment sites.
edges.source: PMP22 Haploinsufficiency and Junctional Myelin Instability

edges.description: Focal conduction abnormalities at entrapment sites manifest clinically as recurrent sensory and motor mononeuropathies.
Motor involvement in focal pressure palsies produces transient or residual weakness in the affected nerve distribution.
Compression-sensitive myelin dysfunction is detected electrophysiologically as reduced motor nerve conduction velocity.

edges.target: PMP22 Haploinsufficiency and Junctional Myelin Instability

nodes.description: Peripheral nerves exposed to ordinary entrapment sites develop recurrent focal sensory and motor neuropathies. Electrodiagnostic testing typically shows focal conduction abnormalities at compression sites, and some patients also show a mild generalized sensorimotor polyneuropathy.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies.
evidence.snippet: Hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies (HNPP) is characterized by recurrent sensory and motor neuropathy in individual nerves starting in adolescence or young adulthood, focal conduction abnormalities at entrapment sites on nerve conduction studies, and sausage-like swellings (tomacula) of the myelin sheaths by nerve biopsy.

evidence.reference_title: Abnormal junctions and permeability of myelin in PMP22-deficient nerves.
evidence.snippet: Our study reveals a novel mechanism by which PMP22 deficiency affects nerve conduction not through removal of myelin, but through disruption of myelin junctions.

nodes.description: Nerve conduction studies show reduced motor conduction velocity, reflecting compression-sensitive peripheral myelin dysfunction.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0003431 | Decreased motor nerve conduction velocity | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Motor deficits occur in the distribution of affected nerves during pressure palsies and may leave mild residual weakness if recovery is incomplete.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary Neuropathy with Liability to Pressure Palsies.
evidence.snippet: The most common initial manifestation is the acute onset of a non-painful focal sensory and motor neuropathy in a single nerve (mononeuropathy).

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies (Orphanet structured-database record)

nodes.description: Heterozygous PMP22 deletion or loss-of-function variation lowers PMP22 dosage in Schwann cells. Reduced PMP22 destabilizes tight and adherens junction architecture within compact peripheral myelin, increasing myelin permeability and making nerves unusually susceptible to mechanical compression.
nodes.id: PMP22 Haploinsufficiency and Junctional Myelin Instability

evidence.reference_title: DNA deletion associated with hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies.
evidence.snippet: The deleted region appears uniform in all pedigrees and includes the gene for peripheral myelin protein 22 (PMP-22), suggesting that underexpression of PMP-22 may cause HNPP.

evidence.reference_title: Abnormal junctions and permeability of myelin in PMP22-deficient nerves.
evidence.snippet: We show disruption of multiple types of cell junction complexes in peripheral nerve, resulting in increased permeability of myelin and impaired action potential propagation.

meta.association: Causal deletion or pathogenic variant leading to PMP22 haploinsufficiency

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary Neuropathy with Liability to Pressure Palsies.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis of HNPP is established in a proband with suggestive clinical and electrophysiologic findings and either the 1.5-Mb recurrent deletion or a novel deletion involving PMP22 (in 80%), or a PMP22 sequence variant (in 20%) identified by molecular genetic testing.

evidence.reference_title: DNA deletion associated with hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies.
evidence.snippet: The deletion in HNPP spans approximately 1.5 Mb and includes all markers that are known to map within the Charcot-Marie-Tooth neuropathy type 1A (CMT1A) duplication.

evidence.reference_title: PMP22 / hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: PMP22 | HGNC:9118 | hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies | MONDO:0008087 | AD | Definitive

nodes.description: Recurrent acute sensory and motor neuropathy affecting single or multiple nerves, often after minor compression, is the typical presenting manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary Neuropathy with Liability to Pressure Palsies.
evidence.snippet: Hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies (HNPP) is characterized by recurrent acute sensory and motor neuropathy in a single or multiple nerves.

evidence.reference_title: Hereditary neuropathy with liability to pressure palsies (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: recurrent mononeuropathies usually triggered by minor physical activities innocuous to healthy people
